steps.action: Check WebSocket URL construction in start()
Read ws.onmessage handler for brainstorm_artifact type
steps.expected: Handler checks data.type === 'brainstorm_artifact' and updates artifact state with filename and content
steps.observed: Handler at line ~82: if (data.type === 'brainstorm_artifact') calls upsertArtifact(data.filename, { filename: data.filename, content: data.content, mimeType: 'text/markdown', updatedAt: new Date().toISOString() })

steps.action: Verify upsertArtifact function updates Map state
steps.expected: Function sets artifact by filename key in artifacts Map
steps.observed: upsertArtifact creates new Map from prev, calls next.set(filename, artifact), returns next — correctly uses immutable Map update
useState<Map<string, BrainstormArtifact>>(new Map()) — correct type with filename key

steps.action: Read ws.onmessage handler for brainstorm_image type
steps.expected: Handler checks data.type === 'brainstorm_image' and stores base64 image data in artifact state
steps.observed: Handler at line ~90: `else if (data.type === 'brainstorm_image')` calls upsertArtifact(data.filename, { filename: data.filename, content: data.data, mimeType: 'image/png', label: data.label, updatedAt: ... })

steps.action: Verify image data is stored as content field
steps.observed: content: data.data — base64 image data stored correctly

steps.action: Verify mimeType is set to image/png

codeLocations: frontend/src/hooks/useGeminiBrainstorm.ts: BrainstormArtifact type — content field stores base64 data

steps.expected: A ref stores the resumption handle
steps.observed: sessionHandleRef = useRef<string | null>(null) — declared at line ~32

steps.expected: Handler stores incoming handle in ref
steps.observed: else if (data.type === 'session_resumption_update') { if (data.handle) { sessionHandleRef.current = data.handle } } — stores new handle

steps.expected: On WebSocket open, send stored handle if available
steps.observed: ws.onopen: if (sessionHandleRef.current) { ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'session_config', handle: sessionHandleRef.current })) } — sends handle on reconnect

codeLocations: frontend/src/hooks/useGeminiBrainstorm.ts: onopen handler sends session_config with handle

assertions.title: Hook provides audio capture and playback

steps.expected: Hook imports shared audio utilities (createAudioContext, requestMicrophone, float32ToPcm16, pcm16ToFloat32, etc.)
steps.observed: Imports: API_BASE_URL, AUDIO_BUFFER_SIZE, MIC_TARGET_RATE, calculateIntensity, createAudioContext, decodeHexAudio, float32ToPcm16, pcm16ToFloat32, requestMicrophone, resampleAudio, scheduleAudioPlayback — all from '../utils/audio.ts'

steps.action: Verify audio capture setup in start()
steps.expected: Uses createAudioContext, requestMicrophone, ScriptProcessorNode with float32ToPcm16 and resampleAudio
steps.observed: start() calls: audioContextRef.current = createAudioContext(); streamRef.current = await requestMicrophone(); creates ScriptProcessorNode with AUDIO_BUFFER_SIZE; onaudioprocess uses resampleAudio(inputData, sourceRate, MIC_TARGET_RATE) and float32ToPcm16(resampledData), sends pcmData.buffer over WebSocket

steps.expected: Uses decodeHexAudio, pcm16ToFloat32, scheduleAudioPlayback for incoming audio
steps.observed: onmessage audio handler: decodeHexAudio(data.content) → pcm16ToFloat32(pcmData) → calculateIntensity(floatData) → scheduleAudioPlayback(audioContextRef.current, floatData, nextPlayTimeRef)

steps.expected: All audio functions imported, not re-implemented
steps.observed: No audio encoding/decoding/resampling code in the hook file — all delegated to utils/audio.ts imports

codeLocations: frontend/src/hooks/useGeminiBrainstorm.ts: start() — audio setup using createAudioContext, requestMicrophone, ScriptProcessorNode
frontend/src/utils/audio.ts: all imported functions defined here

summary: Tested 5 assertions: all 5 passed. The useGeminiBrainstorm hook correctly connects to /api/v1/live/brainstorm, handles brainstorm_artifact and brainstorm_image messages with proper state updates, manages session resumption tokens (store on receive, send on reconnect), and reuses all audio utilities from utils/audio.ts without duplication.
